data.title: Old Republic International posts $330.0M Q1 net income; operating EPS $0.68, premiums up 7.1%
data.description:
Old Republic International reported first-quarter 2026 net income of $330.0 million and net operating income (net income excluding investment gains) of $170.5 million. Consolidated net premiums and fees earned rose to $1.97 billion (up 7.1% year-over-year) while net operating diluted EPS was $0.68. The company returned $237.5 million of capital to shareholders and reported book value per share of $24.53 as of March 31, 2026.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income attributable to shareholders: $330.0 million for Q1 2026 (vs. $245.0 million a year ago).
  • Net income excluding investment gains (net operating income): $170.5 million for Q1 2026 (vs. $201.7 million).
  • Net operating diluted EPS: $0.68 for Q1 2026 (vs. $0.81 prior year); diluted net income per share: $1.32 (vs. $0.98).
  • Consolidated net premiums and fees earned: $1,972.2 million, up 7.1% year-over-year.
  • Net investment income: $178.0 million for Q1 2026 (up from $170.7 million); total net investment gains (pre-tax) were $201.8 million.

Business Highlights

  • Specialty Insurance: net premiums earned increased 4.7% driven by rate increases, new business production and contributions from new operating companies; notable premium growth in commercial auto, property, accident & health, and general liability.
  • Specialty underwriting: segment produced underwriting income but saw combined ratio rise to 94.8% (from 89.8%) due to higher loss and expense ratios, including increased investment in start-up operating companies and IT modernization.
  • Title Insurance: net premiums and fees earned rose 12.0% with stronger commercial business (commercial premiums 27% of title premiums vs. 24% prior year) and improved pretax operating income in the segment.
  • Operational initiatives: continued investments in information technology, data analytics and AI across operating companies; several modernization projects entering amortization phases while legacy systems remain active.
  • Strategic activity: previously announced proposed acquisition of Everett Cash Mutual (ECM) received regulatory approval and is expected to close early in Q3 2026 following policyholder approval and customary conditions; ECM will be reported within Specialty Insurance upon closing.

Original SEC Filing: OLD REPUBLIC INTERNATIONAL CORP [ ORI ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Independent Bank reports Q1 2026 net income $16.9M, $0.81 diluted EPS; NIM 3.65%
data.description:
Independent Bank reported first-quarter 2026 net income of $16.9 million, or $0.81 per diluted share, up from $15.6 million, or $0.74 per diluted share a year earlier. Net interest income was $46.9 million with a taxable-equivalent net interest margin of 3.65%, and non-interest income totaled $12.0 million for the quarter. The bank also recorded core deposit growth and loan growth while maintaining solid capital ratios and an increased tangible common equity ratio of 8.7%.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income: $16.9 million for Q1 2026 ($0.81 diluted EPS).
  • Net interest income: $46.9 million for Q1 2026; net interest margin (FTE) 3.65%.
  • Non-interest income: $12.0 million for Q1 2026.
  • Non-interest expense: $38.3 million for Q1 2026; includes $1.5 million litigation expense and $0.3 million merger-related expense.
  • Tangible common equity per share: $23.38; tangible common equity ratio: 8.71% (tangible common equity ratio excluding AOCI: 9.61%).

Business Highlights

  • Deposit growth: Total deposits rose to $4.88 billion at March 31, 2026, driven by increases in savings, interest-bearing checking, reciprocal and brokered time deposits.
  • Loan growth: Total loans (excl. loans held for sale) increased to $4.31 billion at March 31, 2026, with net loan growth of $31.8 million (3.0% annualized) from December 31, 2025 and $53.8 million (9.9% annualized) growth in commercial loans for the quarter.
  • Mortgage servicing activity: Capitalized mortgage servicing rights totaled $32.2 million at March 31, 2026; mortgage loan servicing, net generated $1.6 million driven by fair value changes.
  • Asset quality: Total non-performing loans were $27.6 million (0.64% of portfolio loans); allowance for credit losses for loans was $63.7 million (1.48% of total portfolio loans).
  • Liquidity & funding: Cash and cash equivalents of $174.9 million and unused credit lines with the FHLB and FRB totaling approximately $785.5 million and $1.36 billion, respectively; approximately $440.7 million of unpledged securities AFS and HTM available for additional borrowing capacity.

Original SEC Filing: INDEPENDENT BANK CORP /MI/ [ IBCP ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Applied Digital secures $7.5B, 300 MW 15-year hyperscaler lease; total contracted revenue tops $23B
data.description:
Applied Digital announced a new 15-year lease worth about $7.5 billion for 300 MW at Delta Forge 1, bringing total contracted lease revenue to over $23 billion.

Key Highlights:

  • Entered lease with a U.S. high investment-grade hyperscaler for 300 MW at Delta Forge 1 — ~15-year term, ~$7.5B total contracted value.
  • New tenant is Applied Digital’s second U.S. investment-grade hyperscaler and third hyperscale customer, diversifying revenue base.
  • Total contracted lease revenue across campuses now exceeds $23B; >50% of contracted revenue backed by investment-grade customers.
  • Updated investor presentation posted and furnished; Delta Forge 1 initial operations expected to begin mid-2027.
  • Company expects to enter up to $300M senior secured bridge and up to $300M senior secured revolving credit facilities to support development and working capital.

Original SEC Filing: Applied Digital Corp. [ APLD ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

data.title: ZAWYA: Rently: Tackling the challenges of modern rentals
data.description:
Dubai, United Arab Emirates, Rently, a rental technology platform in the UAE, continues to expand its footprint in the UAE, streamlining how rent is paid by enabling flexible monthly payments for tenants while supporting structured and timely payouts to landlords. Rently is designed for a rapidly evolving rental market, where changing rental costs and payment structures are driving demand for more efficient, digital-first solutions.

Rently sits at the intersection of payments, property management and tenant services, enabling tenants to pay rent in manageable monthly payments while ensuring landlords receive payments according to their preferred schedule. The platform reduces operational roadblocks for property managers and provides secure, compliant and seamless digital solutions.

It supports rental payment flows end-to-end, including collections, payouts and reconciliation, reducing administrative overhead and improving payment consistency across the leasing lifecycle. By offering flexible monthly payments based on the number of payouts, Rently empowers tenants to secure homes with confidence while managing their cashflow.

Dominic Schacher, co-founder of Rently, said, “Our model is tested, proven, and designed to scale across markets. Since establishing operations in the UAE, we have seen great progress in the past few months, and we remain committed to driving innovation, supporting tenants and delivering certainty for landlords across the region.”

The platform is relevant to a broad range of renters across the UAE, from those new to the market and accustomed to monthly payment structures, to long-term residents seeking greater flexibility and control over how they manage rent. Users also benefit from a growing range of innovative features such as rental rewards programs and UAE Pass digital integration.

Landlords gain from predictable rental income, reduced collection risk, and a more streamlined leasing process without requiring changes to existing payment structures. For agents and property managers, it simplifies collections, reduces follow-ups, and supports faster deal closures by removing friction in the rental process. This approach not only makes renting more accessible but also encourages long-term engagement with the rental ecosystem, supporting both tenants and landlords as the market grows.

Rently was founded by a team of entrepreneurs with experience across technology, payments, and finance, focused on improving how rent is paid and managed across markets including Singapore and the UAE. Following its initial rollout in Dubai, Rently is now expanding its presence across the UAE, working with a growing network of property agencies and partners including White & Co, Haus & Haus and Betterhomes. The platform has already processed over $10 million in rental transactions in the UAE, reflecting growing demand for more flexible and reliable rental payment solutions. The company has also established a dedicated local team to support its operations and long-term growth in the region.

Taimur Khan, Managing Director UAE at Rently, added, “Since launching in Dubai, we have seen strong interest from partners across the UAE, reflecting clear demand for more flexible and streamlined rental payment solutions. As we expand, our focus is on building deep relationships on the ground and delivering solutions that work for the realities of the local market.”

Shiv Mahajan, CEO of Rently, commented, “Rent is one of the largest financial commitments people make, yet the experience around it needs to evolve further. We are focused on making renting simpler, more flexible and more rewarding, while bringing greater consistency and transparency to landlords and property managers.”

By addressing the structural evolution of renting and combining flexibility, certainty and operational expertise, Rently UAE is redefining rental commerce for the modern, digital-first economy.

For more information, please visit rently-uae.com or follow @rentlyuae.

About Rently

Rently UAE is a rental technology platform modernising how rent works across the UAE, enabling flexible monthly payments for tenants while supporting structured and timely payouts to landlords. By supporting rental payment flows, collections, and reconciliation, Rently helps reduce friction in the leasing process and improves cashflow for both tenants and property owners. Active in Singapore and the UAE, Rently works with landlords, property managers, and partners to streamline rental payments and modernise the rental experience, making renting simpler, more flexible and more rewarding. With a growing network of agency and landlord partnerships, a continuously evolving product offering, and a strong commitment to stability and resilience, Rently is redefining rental commerce in a modern, digital-first economy.

Visit rently-uae.com or follow @rentlyuae for more details.

For any questions, please reach out to rently@ruderfinnatteline.com.

Send us your press releases to pressrelease.zawya@lseg.com

data.title: Lockheed Burns Through Cash; Stock Falls — WSJ
data.description:
By Drew FitzGerald

Lockheed Martin shares are slipping 3.5% premarket after the defense contractor burned through more cash than expected over the winter months.

  • The company reported $291 million of negative free cash flow in its most recent quarter, during what is supposed to be a bumper year for the maker of missiles and fighter jets. Lockheed still predicts a positive year with free cash flow between $6.5 billion and $6.8 billion in 2026.
  • The company blamed the timing of some billings, and some $1 billion spent on long-term debt repayments plus $816 million on cash dividends also took a bite.

Lockheed Martin looks set to join peers RTX and Northrop Grumman who are also seeing their shares sell off so far this week despite a war in the Middle East that's boosting demand for their products. Higher fuel prices and stepped-up investments in production are damping investor enthusiasm.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: ZAWYA: Celebrating marketing that works: Asia's 26-year-old Dragons Awards expands into MENA
data.description:
Dubai, UAE: Dragons of MENA, a results-focused marketing communications awards programme built on 26 years of credibility, is debuting in Dubai.

The programme is an extension of the PMAA Dragons of Asia Awards, established in 2000 and recognised as one of the industry's most respected results-focused awards programmes, with satellite programmes already running in Malaysia, Pakistan and Sri Lanka. It has been created in response to growing demand from agencies and brand owners across the region.

The Dragons of Asia have been acknowledged by WARC alongside leading global effectiveness awards, including Cannes Lions, the IPA Effectiveness Awards and the Effies.

In its inaugural year, Dragons of MENA will feature 10 categories: eight core categories drawn from the Dragons of Asia alongside two additional categories designed to reflect regional priorities and context, including Nation, City & Place Impact and Faith, and Arabic Culture & Seasonal Communications. All entries are judged equally across four criteria: strategy, concept, execution and results.

Mike Da Silva, Founder of The Dragons of Asia said: "The Gulf and wider Middle East represent some of the fastest-growing and most creatively vibrant markets in the world and expanding the Dragons here is a significant moment for us.

"Our ambition is to bring the entire creative community together, from long-established consultancies to the independent agencies doing exceptional work that too often goes unrecognised. Dragons of MENA exists to change that."

A Platform for Work That Delivers

The MENA region has long been producing world-class marketing communications work, much of it underrepresented in global awards. Unlike programmes that celebrate craft and creativity alone, Dragons of MENA recognises campaigns that deliver measurable business results: sales, behaviour change, brand growth, loyalty and acquisition.

Entries are open to agencies of all sizes, brand owners, government entities, NGOs and in-house marketing teams across the MENA region, including the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Egypt, Morocco, Qatar, Kuwait and beyond. Eligible campaigns must have run between 1 January 2025 and 15 June, 2026.

The programme fills a gap for disciplines such as promotional marketing, PR, experiential, activation and shopper marketing, where results matter as much as the creative idea.

The Meaning of the Dragon

In Asia, the Dragon is a symbol of strength, wisdom and prosperity. In the MENA region, the symbolism carries a different nuance: the Dragon represents a force to be faced, challenged and ultimately conquered. To earn a Dragon here is not simply to win. It is to overcome.

Accessible by Design

A single entry fee of AU$200 (approximately US$140) covers submission to both Dragons of MENA and the Dragons of Asia Awards, offering participants regional and pan-Asian recognition through a single entry.

Winners will gain global visibility through AdForum, the Dragons of MENA Media Partner.

Entries are open now across all 10 categories, with submissions closing on 15 June 2026 on

https://dragonsofasia.org/mena

The Dubai awards event is planned for September, with further details on the venue and event partners to follow.

About Dragons of MENA

Dragons of MENA is an extension of the PMAA Dragons of Asia Awards, bringing the programme's commitment to results-driven marketing communications to the Middle East and North Africa for the first time. Entries are judged on strategy, concept, execution and results, all weighted equally. Winners automatically qualify to compete in the Dragons of Asia, where the highest-scoring campaigns from across Asia and MENA compete to win Dragons of Asia: Red, Blue, Gold, Silver & Black Dragons, awarded in Kuala Lumpur in October. The Dragons of MENA is powered by Unthinkable Creative Co. LCC, United Arab Emirates.

https://dragonsofasia.org/mena

About Mike Da Silva

Mike Da Silva is CEO of Sydney-based MDSA Marketing Communications, one of Australia's most awarded agencies, with over four decades' experience in the UK, Australia and Asia.

A specialist in tourism and movie marketing, his clients include Qantas (42 years), ANZ Bank, Accor, Foxtel Cable Network, PayPal and Viking Cruises. He created and owned the Qantas Movie Club, launching 114 films to 15 million frequent flyers over 9 years, and is Executive Producer (Marketing) for several movies.

Mike is an internationally recognised marketer and UK & Australia industry pioneer, with a number of global awards, including twin Hall of Fame honours. (USA & Australia).

In 2000, Mike founded the Dragons of Asia Awards to recognise marketing communications work not previously seen in the west. The Dragons is Asia's elite results-focused marketing communications programme, with satellite extensions into Malaysia, Pakistan, Sri Lanka and now MENA.

Media Contact

Hannah Baird, MSL, Hannah.baird@mslgroup.co

Send us your press releases to pressrelease.zawya@lseg.com

data.title: Freeport-McMoRan beats first-quarter profit on higher copper prices
data.description:
Miner Freeport-McMoRan beat Wall Street estimates for first-quarter profit on Thursday, powered by higher copper prices.

Average copper prices soared 36.7% in the first quarter from a year earlier and hit all-time highs late in January on supply constraints, low inventory and strong demand.

Quarterly average realized price for copper was $5.78 per pound, the company said, compared with $4.44 per pound a year earlier.

The company reported an adjusted profit of 57 cents per share for the three months ended March 31, compared with analysts' average estimate of 46 cents, according to data compiled by LSEG.

data.title: Carter Bankshares Q1 EPS beats estimates on loan sale gain
data.description:
Overview

  • US regional bank's Q1 adjusted EPS beat analyst expectations, driven by gain on loan sale

  • Net interest income for Q1 rose 19% yr/yr

  • Nonperforming loans fell sharply after sale of nonaccrual credits

Outlook

  • Company expects continued momentum in commercial and small business lending platforms

  • Company anticipates prior construction lending commitments to fund over next 12-18 months

  • Company says balance sheet remains positioned to benefit from potential Fed rate reductions

Result Drivers

  • LOAN SALE GAIN - Q1 results were driven by an $80 mln net gain from the sale of nonperforming loans, which included a $65 mln transaction gain and $15 mln net recovery

  • CREDIT RESERVE RELEASE - Release of $18 mln in specific reserves related to the sold loans contributed to higher earnings and lower allowance for credit losses

  • NET INTEREST MARGIN EXPANSION - Net interest income and margin increased due to lower funding costs and higher average loan balances

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at close
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex shed 1.09% to 77,664 and the broader NSE Nifty 50 index lost 0.84% to 24,173.05, dragged by financials and automakers as Brent crude topped $100 a barrel after Iran seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz and peace talks showed no sign of resuming.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee dropped 0.3% versus the U.S. dollar to 94.1050, falling for a fourth straight session on Thursday, its longest losing streak since mid-January, as stalled peace talks between the U.S. and Iran and oil prices over $100 per barrel weighed on sentiment.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN064835G=CC) was quoted at rupees, with the yield up 2.73 bps at 6.9498%, as oil prices vaulted past $100 a barrel on the U.S.-Iran standoff, bruising sentiment in oil-import-reliant nation.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was up 7 basis points at 5.8950%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) rose 7.25 bps to 6.49%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.10% and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5%.

data.title: United Bankshares reports Q1 2026 net income $124.2M, EPS $0.89; NIM 3.80%
data.description:
United Bankshares reported first-quarter 2026 net income of $124.2 million, or $0.89 per diluted share, compared with $84.3 million, or $0.59 per diluted share, in Q1 2025. The company recorded net interest income of $282.5 million and a net interest margin of 3.80% for the quarter. Return on average assets and return on average shareholders’ equity were 1.49% and 9.08%, respectively.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income (GAAP): $124.2 million for Q1 2026 (Q1 2025: $84.3 million).
  • Diluted earnings per share: $0.89 for Q1 2026 (Q1 2025: $0.59).
  • Net interest income: $282.5 million for Q1 2026.
  • Net interest margin: 3.80% for Q1 2026 (Q1 2025: 3.69%).
  • Provision for credit losses: $7.8 million for Q1 2026; allowance for loan & lease losses ending balance $299.6 million (1.20% of loans & leases).

Business Highlights

  • Acquisition integration: First-quarter results reflect prior acquisition of Piedmont completed January 10, 2025; Q1 2025 included merger-related charges and provision impacts associated with Piedmont.
  • Asset quality: Non-performing loans were $102.8 million (0.41% of loans & leases) and total non-performing assets $113.2 million (0.34% of total assets) at March 31, 2026.
  • Loan growth: Average net loans and loans held for sale increased approximately $1.4 billion (6%) versus Q1 2025.
  • Deposit and funding: Total deposits were $27.12 billion at quarter end; average rate paid on interest-bearing deposits declined year-over-year, aiding net interest income expansion.
  • Capital management: Estimated risk-based capital ratio 15.5% and Common Equity Tier 1 ratio 13.3% at March 31, 2026; repurchased ~1.7 million shares in Q1 under existing repurchase plan.

Original SEC Filing: UNITED BANKSHARES INC/WV [ UBSI ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

data.title: SmartKem Disclosed Failure to Satisfy a Continued Listing Rule or Standard
data.description:
On April 21, 2026, Nasdaq's Hearings Panel notified the company that it regained compliance with the stockholders' equity rule, but it remains out of compliance with the minimum bid price rule and has until September 1, 2026 to regain compliance. The company will be subject to a one-year Discretionary Panel Monitor; any new noncompliance during this period would trigger an immediate delist determination, appealable to a Panel.

Original SEC Filing: SmartKem, Inc. [ SMTK ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

data.title: Sight Sciences wins $55.4M patent judgment, 10% ongoing royalty on Hydrus sales
data.description:
Sight Sciences announced a final judgment awarding $55.4 million and a 10% ongoing royalty related to willful patent infringement by Alcon.

Key Highlights:

  • Final Judgment upholds jury finding that three Sight Sciences patents were valid and willfully infringed by Alcon.
  • Monetary award totals $55.4 million: $34.0M past damages, $14.7M supplemental damages, $6.8M interest.
  • Ongoing 10% royalty on Hydrus revenue through expiration of the last asserted patent.
  • Award is subject to Alcon appeal; no funds received until appeals are exhausted or time to appeal lapses.

Original SEC Filing: Sight Sciences, Inc. [ SGHT ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

data.title: Republic Bancorp reports Q1 2026 net income $42.6M; adjusted EPS $2.04
data.description:
Republic Bancorp reported first-quarter 2026 net income of $42.6 million and diluted earnings per Class A share of $2.18, driven by Core Bank net interest income expansion. Adjusted net income (non-GAAP) rose to $39.9 million, with adjusted diluted EPS of $2.04, reflecting exclusion of several nonrecurring items. Management highlighted strong Traditional Banking and Warehouse Lending performance that more than offset a TRS contract nonrenewal and an early FHLB repayment penalty.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income (GAAP) for Q1 2026: $42.6 million; Q1 2025: $47.3 million (decrease of $4.7 million, -10%).
  • Diluted earnings per Class A common share (GAAP): $2.18 in Q1 2026; $2.42 in Q1 2025 (down $0.24, -10%).
  • Adjusted Net Income (Non-GAAP): $39.9 million for Q1 2026 versus $38.9 million for Q1 2025 (increase of $1.0 million, +3%).
  • Adjusted Diluted EPS (Non-GAAP): $2.04 for Q1 2026 versus $1.99 for Q1 2025 (increase of $0.05, +3%).
  • Core Bank net interest income: $63.2 million in Q1 2026, up $6.9 million (+12%) from $56.3 million in Q1 2025; Core Bank net interest margin rose 26 bps to 3.96%.

Business Highlights

  • Core Bank momentum: Traditional Banking and Warehouse Lending were primary drivers of performance, delivering net interest income and margin expansion through deposit cost declines and higher-yield investments.
  • Warehouse Lending growth: Average outstanding warehouse lines increased 33% year-over-year to $610 million, with committed lines up to $1.22 billion and usage rising to ~50%.
  • Liquidity deployment: Excess cash was shifted into longer-term investment securities, raising average investments to $907 million with a 4.24% yield, while average interest-earning cash declined.
  • RPG segmentation: Republic Processing Group net income declined 37% year-over-year to $18.8 million, primarily due to the nonrenewal of a large Tax Refund Solutions contract; TRS revenues remain concentrated in Q1–Q2 seasonality.
  • Credit and expense trends: Core Bank credit provision was a net charge of $0.4 million for Q1 2026; adjusted noninterest expenses for the Core Bank were essentially flat year-over-year, reflecting cost savings from the core conversion and disciplined expense management.

Original SEC Filing: REPUBLIC BANCORP INC /KY/ [ RBCAA ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026
